description.value:
The third dance is used to deal with the user's foe's as easy as possible, by scaring them away. This technique can only be used while the user is in the Meimu no Kamae stance. This technique causes the user's nerves to tense and get an undeniable sense of something bad about to happen. Non-heroic characters are assumed to fail this save, and are instantly panicked. Any other creature that succeeds it's save feels very uneasy and suffers from being shaken. A creature that fails it's saved instead becomes frightened.

After 1d4 rounds, if the user is still performing the dance, the sounds and speed of the dance increase greatly. Each creature must succeed the save or rise one level in fear (Shaken->Frightened->Panicked).

Creatures outside 30 feet, but withing range gain a +2 bonus to both saves. In the first part of the dance, the user gains a +1 Dodge Bonus to defense until they stop dancing. Or, if the dance continues into the second part, this bonus increases of +2.

Requires: Taijutsu 9 ranks; Meimu no Kamae(t)

Material Focus: To accurately perform this technique, the user needs specially made clothes, usually equipped with whistles or bells that cause the illusion. These clothes usually have a Cost DC of 10.
